title: Financial Stability Review
summary: The ECB's Financial Stability Review, published twice yearly, provides an overview of potential risks to euro area financial stability. The November 2025 edition identifies elevated financial stability vulnerabilities driven by uncertainty over geoeconomic trends and tariff impacts. This issue also examines systemic risks arising from linkages between banks and non-bank financial intermediaries.
key_points: The ECB Financial Stability Review is published twice yearly; next release scheduled for 27 May 2026
November 2025 edition identifies elevated financial stability vulnerabilities given geoeconomic uncertainty and tariff impact
A key focus of the latest edition is systemic risk arising from bank to non-bank financial intermediary linkages
The press release states uncertainty over global economic trends and tariffs are keeping financial stability risks elevated
Statistical data and full report available as separate downloads from this landing page
why_it_matters: Financial stability assessments from the ECB provide critical context for macro risk positioning in euro area markets. The elevated vulnerability assessment signals ongoing concern about trade policy uncertainty and its potential knock-on effects to the broader financial system.
macro_context: Elevated financial stability risks stem from geoeconomic uncertainty, tariff impacts, and concerns about bank-to-non-bank linkages. The broad scope across equities, bonds, and forex suggests systemic concern rather than sector-specific risk.

issues_found: Source is a listing/index page (https://www.ecb.europa.eu/press/financial-stability-publications/fsr/html/index.en.html) - Rule 5 requires rejection for listing pages
Source contains only high-level descriptions and navigation links; no substantive report content
No specific data points, quantitative assessments, or risk metrics provided in the source
No forward guidance or specific policy direction stated
The actual November 2025 Financial Stability Review report content is not present - only links to download it
Published date set as 2025-11-01 is an assumption; source only indicates 'November 2025' without a specific day

notes: SOURCE IS A LISTING/INDEX PAGE. This page aggregates multiple Financial Stability Review releases (November 2025, May 2025, etc.) but does not contain the substantive report content. Only summary descriptions and links are provided. The November 2025 release is the most recent edition referenced. For actual risk analysis and data, the full PDF reports should be accessed. The source does not provide quantitative metrics or specific forward guidance, limiting its utility for detailed macro catalyst analysis.
